claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 21 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 15 years old and does not live with you.
  - Charlie is 11 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Claimant contributes £50.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 11 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children under the age of 16, or children aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have children. I have three actually - Alex who is 21, Blake who is 15, and Charlie who is 11.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me ask some follow-up questions to assess your eligibility for each of your children.

Let me start with Alex, who is 21. Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in education. He actually works full-time now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that about Alex.

Now, regarding Blake (who is 15) and Charlie (who is 11) - do they both live with you in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I live in the UK, in Manchester. Charlie lives with me, but Blake doesn't - Blake lives with their mum.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I have a few more questions to help determine your eligibility.

Regarding Blake, who lives with their mum - do you contribute financially to Blake's upkeep? For example, do you pay for things like food, clothes, pocket money, or contribute to their accommodation - at least the equivalent of what the Child Benefit payment would be?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I do help out a bit with Blake - I send some money for clothes and things like that, but it's not much. It's about £10 a week.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just to clarify - do you know approximately how much the Child Benefit payment is per week, and would you say your £10 per week contribution is at least equal to the Child Benefit amount for Blake?

Also, does Blake's mum currently claim Child Benefit for Blake?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm not entirely sure what the Child Benefit amount is exactly, but I'd imagine my £10 a week is probably less than that. And yes, Blake's mum does claim Child Benefit for Blake already.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. That's helpful information.

Now, just to confirm about Charlie who is 11 and lives with you - is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Charlie?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. It's just me looking after Charlie at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, here's my assessment of your Child Benefit eligibility for each of your children:

**Alex (21 years old):** You are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex. Since Alex is over 20 and not in approved education or training (he works full-time), Child Benefit does not apply.

**Blake (15 years old):** You are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Blake. Although Blake is the right age, Blake's mother is already claiming Child Benefit for them, and only one person can claim for the same child. Additionally, your £10 weekly contribution is less than the Child Benefit amount, which would not be sufficient to qualify you for the benefit under the contribution rules.

**Charlie (11 years old):** You **are eligible** for Child Benefit for Charlie! Charlie is under 16, lives with you in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them. You are responsible for Charlie as they live with you.

So in summary, you can claim Child Benefit for Charlie. If you'd like to proceed with claiming, you can make an application for Child Benefit for Charlie.

reasonings: Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or for those aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training. Since Alex is over 20 and working full-time, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 15 and does not live with the claimant. While the claimant contributes approximately £10 per week towards Blake's upkeep, this is less than the Child Benefit amount, which is not sufficient to qualify for Child Benefit under the contribution rules. Additionally, Blake's mother is already claiming Child Benefit for Blake, and only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 11 years old, lives with the claimant in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. The claimant is responsible for Charlie as they live together. Since Charlie is under 16 and all eligibility criteria are met, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has three children: Alex (21), Blake (15), and Charlie (11). Alex is too old and not in approved education, so no eligibility applies. Blake lives with their mother who already claims Child Benefit, and the claimant's £10 weekly contribution is insufficient to qualify for Child Benefit. However, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie, who is 11, lives with them in the UK, and has no other person claiming for them.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or for those aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training. Since Alex is over 20 and working full-time, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 15 and does not live with the claimant. While the claimant contributes approximately £10 per week towards Blake's upkeep, this is less than the Child Benefit amount, which is not sufficient to qualify for Child Benefit under the contribution rules. Additionally, Blake's mother is already claiming Child Benefit for Blake, and only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 11 years old, lives with the claimant in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. The claimant is responsible for Charlie as they live together. Since Charlie is under 16 and all eligibility criteria are met, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Charlie.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has three children: Alex (21), Blake (15), and Charlie (11). Alex is too old and not in approved education, so no eligibility applies. Blake lives with their mother who already claims Child Benefit, and the claimant's £10 weekly contribution is insufficient to qualify for Child Benefit. However, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie, who is 11, lives with them in the UK, and has no other person claiming for them.
